examples.sentence: 天候はどうであれ、試合は行われます。
examples.translation: No matter what the weather is, the match will be held.
examples.translationEn: No matter what the weather is, the match will be held.
examples.romaji: Tenkou wa dou de are, shiai wa okonawaremasu.

examples.sentence: 結果はどうであれ、最善を尽くすだけです。
examples.translation: No matter what the result is, all we can do is try our best.
examples.translationEn: No matter what the result is, all we can do is try our best.
examples.romaji: Kekka wa dou de are, saizen wo tsukusu dake desu.

examples.sentence: あなたの意見はどうであれ、私は彼を信じます。
examples.translation: No matter what your opinion is, I believe him.
examples.translationEn: No matter what your opinion is, I believe him.
examples.romaji: Anata no iken wa dou de are, watashi wa kare wo shinjimasu.

examples.sentence: 試験の結果はどうであれ、あなたには何も変わりません。
examples.translation: No matter what the result of the test is, nothing will change for you.
examples.translationEn: No matter what the result of the test is, nothing will change for you.
examples.romaji: Shiken no kekka wa dou de are, anata ni wa nani mo kawarimasen.

examples.sentence: 彼は泥まみれになって帰ってきた。
examples.translation: He returned home covered in mud.
examples.translationEn: He returned home covered in mud.
examples.romaji: Kare wa doro mamire ni natte kaette kita.

examples.sentence: 彼女は涙まみれになりながら告白した。
examples.translation: She confessed while crying her eyes out.
examples.translationEn: She confessed while crying her eyes out.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa namida mamire ni narinagara kokuhaku shita.

examples.sentence: その事件後、彼はスキャンダルまみれになった。
examples.translation: After that incident, he was completely embroiled in scandal.
examples.translationEn: After that incident, he was completely embroiled in scandal.
examples.romaji: Sono jiken go, kare wa sukandaru mamire ni natta.

examples.sentence: この部屋は埃まみれだ。
examples.translation: This room is covered in dust.
examples.translationEn: This room is covered in dust.
examples.romaji: Kono heya wa hokori mamire da.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun もさることながら Noun も
examples.sentence: 彼の歌唱力もさることながら、ダンスも素晴らしい。
examples.translation: Not only his singing ability but also his dancing is wonderful.
examples.translationEn: Not only his singing ability but also his dancing is wonderful.
examples.romaji: Kare no kashou-ryoku mo saru koto nagara, dansu mo subarashii.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun もさることながら Noun も
examples.sentence: このレストラン、味もさることながら、サービスもすばらしい。
examples.translation: At this restaurant, not only the taste but also the service is wonderful.
examples.translationEn: At this restaurant, not only the taste but also the service is wonderful.
examples.romaji: Kono resutoran, aji mo saru koto nagara, saabisu mo subarashii.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun もさることながら Noun も
examples.sentence: 彼女の美しさもさることながら、その知性も魅力的だ。
examples.translation: Not only her beauty but also her intelligence is attractive.
examples.translationEn: Not only her beauty but also her intelligence is attractive.
examples.romaji: Kanojo no utsukushisa mo saru koto nagara, sono chisei mo miryoku-teki da.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun もさることながら Noun も
examples.sentence: その映画、ストーリーもさることながら、音楽も素晴らしかった。
examples.translation: In that movie, not only the story but also the music was wonderful.
examples.translationEn: In that movie, not only the story but also the music was wonderful.
examples.romaji: Sono eiga, sutoorii mo saru koto nagara, ongaku mo subarashikatta.

examples.sentence: 散歩も兼ねて、公園に行きました。
examples.translation: I went to the park, also for a walk.
examples.translationEn: I went to the park, also for a walk.
examples.romaji: Sanpo mo kanete, kouen ni ikimashita.

examples.sentence: 今日は日本語の勉強も兼ねて、日本の映画を見ます。
examples.translation: Today, I will watch a Japanese movie, also to study Japanese.
examples.translationEn: Today, I will watch a Japanese movie, also to study Japanese.
examples.romaji: Kyou wa Nihongo no benkyou mo kanete, Nihon no eiga wo mimasu.

examples.sentence: 仕事も兼ねて、海外に行く予定です。
examples.translation: I plan to go abroad, also for work.
examples.translationEn: I plan to go abroad, also for work.
examples.romaji: Shigoto mo kanete, kaigai ni iku yotei desu.

examples.sentence: 旅行も兼ねて、友人の結婚式に出席します。
examples.translation: I will attend a friend's wedding, also as a trip.
examples.translationEn: I will attend a friend's wedding, also as a trip.
examples.romaji: Ryokou mo kanete, yuujin no kekkonshiki ni shusseki shimasu.

examples.sentence: 気候も相まって、この地域は農業に適しています。
examples.translation: Combined with the climate, this area is suitable for agriculture.
examples.translationEn: Combined with the climate, this area is suitable for agriculture.
examples.romaji: Kikou mo aimatte, kono chiiki wa nougyou ni tekishiteimasu.

examples.sentence: 彼の才能も相まって、彼は急速に昇進した。
examples.translation: Combined with his talent, he was rapidly promoted.
examples.translationEn: Combined with his talent, he was rapidly promoted.
examples.romaji: Kare no sainou mo aimatte, kare wa kyuusoku ni shoushin shita.

examples.sentence: 技術進歩も相まって、私たちはより効率的に仕事をこなすことができるようになりました。
examples.translation: Combined with technological progress, we have become able to handle our work more efficiently.
examples.translationEn: Combined with technological progress, we have become able to handle our work more efficiently.
examples.romaji: Gijutsu shinpo mo aimatte, watashitachi wa yori kouritsu-teki ni shigoto wo konasu koto ga dekiru you ni narimashita.

examples.sentence: 彼の努力も相まって、プロジェクトは成功しました。
examples.translation: Combined with his efforts, the project was successful.
examples.translationEn: Combined with his efforts, the project was successful.
examples.romaji: Kare no doryoku mo aimatte, purojekuto wa seikou shimashita.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun を おいて他に Verb ない
examples.sentence: この仕事を彼に任せる人をおいて他にいない。
examples.translation: Apart from him, there's no one else to entrust this job to.
examples.translationEn: Apart from him, there's no one else to entrust this job to.
examples.romaji: Kono shigoto wo kare ni makaseru hito wo oite hoka ni inai.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun を おいて他に Verb ない
examples.sentence: この問題を解決できるのは彼をおいて他にいない。
examples.translation: Apart from him, there's no one else who can solve this problem.
examples.translationEn: Apart from him, there's no one else who can solve this problem.
examples.romaji: Kono mondai wo kaiketsu dekiru no wa kare o oite hoka ni inai.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun を おいて他に Verb ない
examples.sentence: この地域で美味しい麺を作る店をおいて他にない。
examples.translation: There is no other restaurant in this area that makes delicious noodles apart from this one.
examples.translationEn: There is no other restaurant in this area that makes delicious noodles apart from this one.
examples.romaji: Kono chiiki de oishii men o tsukuru mise o oite hoka ni nai.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun を おいて他に Verb ない
examples.sentence: 彼女の歌声を超える歌手をおいて他にいない。
examples.translation: There is no other singer who could surpass her singing voice.
examples.translationEn: There is no other singer who could surpass her singing voice.
examples.romaji: Kanojo no utagoe o koeru kashu o oite hoka ni inai.

examples.sentence: 彼は誠実さをもって人々に信頼されています。
examples.translation: He is trusted by people with his honesty.
examples.translationEn: He is trusted by people with his honesty.
examples.romaji: Kare wa seijitsusa wo motte hitobito ni shinrai sarete imasu.

examples.sentence: この賞をもって、彼女の努力が認められました。
examples.translation: With this award, her efforts were recognized.
examples.translationEn: With this award, her efforts were recognized.
examples.romaji: Kono shou wo motte, kanojo no doryoku ga mitomeraremashita.

examples.sentence: 彼は強い決意をもって問題に取り組んだ。
examples.translation: He tackled the problem with strong determination.
examples.translationEn: He tackled the problem with strong determination.
examples.romaji: Kare wa tsuyoi ketsui wo motte mondai ni torikunda.

examples.sentence: 3月をもって、この店は閉店します。
examples.translation: This store will close at the end of March.
examples.translationEn: This store will close at the end of March.
examples.romaji: Sangatsu wo motte, kono mise wa heiten shimasu.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun をものともせずに
examples.sentence: 彼女は怖さをものともせずに、暗闇の中へ進んで行った。
examples.translation: Despite her fear, she went ahead into the darkness.
examples.translationEn: Despite her fear, she went ahead into the darkness.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa kowasa wo mono tomo sezu ni, kurayami no naka e susunde itta.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun をものともせずに
examples.sentence: 試験のプレッシャーをものともせずに、彼は自信満々に答えた。
examples.translation: Unfazed by the pressure of the exam, he answered with full confidence.
examples.translationEn: Unfazed by the pressure of the exam, he answered with full confidence.
examples.romaji: Shiken no puresshaa wo mono tomo sezu ni, kare wa jishin manman ni kotaeta.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun をものともせずに
examples.sentence: 彼は風雪をものともせずに山を登った。
examples.translation: He climbed the mountain unfazed by the wind and snow.
examples.translationEn: He climbed the mountain unfazed by the wind and snow.
examples.romaji: Kare wa fuusetsu wo mono tomo sezu ni, yama wo nobotta.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun をものともせずに
examples.sentence: 彼は失敗をものともせずに、前に進み続けた。
examples.translation: He continued to move forward, undeterred by failure.
examples.translationEn: He continued to move forward, undeterred by failure.
examples.romaji: Kare wa shippai wo mono tomo sezu ni, mae ni susumi tsuzuketa.

examples.sentence: 経済危機をよそに、彼は高級な車を買った。
examples.translation: Despite the economic crisis, he bought a luxury car.
examples.translationEn: Despite the economic crisis, he bought a luxury car.
examples.romaji: Keizai kiki wo yoso ni, kare wa koukyuu na kuruma wo katta.

examples.sentence: 試験が迫るをよそに、彼は遊び続けている。
examples.translation: Despite the approaching test, he keeps on playing.
examples.translationEn: Despite the approaching test, he keeps on playing.
examples.romaji: Shiken ga semaru wo yoso ni, kare wa asobi tsuzukete iru.

examples.sentence: 親の反対をよそに、彼女は彼と結婚しました。
examples.translation: Despite her parents' opposition, she married him.
examples.translationEn: Despite her parents' opposition, she married him.
examples.romaji: Oya no hantai wo yoso ni, kanojo wa kare to kekkon shimashita.

examples.sentence: その国の経済状況をよそに、多くの企業が進出し続けている。
examples.translation: Despite the country's economic situation, many companies continue to expand.
examples.translationEn: Despite the country's economic situation, many companies continue to expand.
examples.romaji: Sono kuni no keizai joukyou wo yoso ni, ooku no kigyou ga shinshutsu shi tsuzukete iru.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun を余儀なくされる
examples.sentence: 彼は経済的困難により、退学を余儀なくされた。
examples.translation: Due to financial difficulties, he was forced to drop out of school.
examples.translationEn: Due to financial difficulties, he was forced to drop out of school.
examples.romaji: Kare wa keizai-teki konnan ni yori, taigaku wo yogi naku saremashita.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun を余儀なくされる
examples.sentence: 厳しい天候によって、私たちは遠足を中止する余儀なくされた。
examples.translation: We were forced to cancel the excursion due to the severe weather.
examples.translationEn: We were forced to cancel the excursion due to the severe weather.
examples.romaji: Kibishi tenkou ni yotte, watashitachi wa ensoku wo chushi suru yogi naku sareta.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun を余儀なくされる
examples.sentence: 怪我のため、彼は入院を余儀なくされた。
examples.translation: Due to his injury, he was forced to be hospitalized.
examples.translationEn: Due to his injury, he was forced to be hospitalized.
examples.romaji: Kega no tame, kare wa nyuuin wo yogi naku sareta.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun を余儀なくされる
examples.sentence: リストラの恐怖により、多くの従業員が転職を余儀なくされた。
examples.translation: Fear of layoffs forced many employees to change jobs.
examples.translationEn: Fear of layoffs forced many employees to change jobs.
examples.romaji: Risutora no kyoufu ni yori, ooku no juugyouin ga tenshoku wo yogi naku sareta.
